blockingIssues.description: Version-metadata violations remain unresolved across the rerun fixes: `fix-runtime-messages-test-coverage`, `fix-profile-test-coverage`, and `fix-json-rpc-test-coverage` each bundled unrelated package/manifest version bumps, and `fix-version-bump-issues` still leaves `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` at `0.4.61` instead of the documented pre-refactor baseline `0.4.60`.

The original `shared-consolidate-profile-types` blocker is still unresolved: `packages/shared/src/provider-instance.ts` does not make `ProviderInstance` truly share the extracted base shape with `ProfileConfig`, so the rerun added evidence without fixing the underlying type-sharing mismatch.
The rerun adds two profile suites over the mission hard limit: `tests/unit/suites/profile/core.test.ts` (240 lines) and `tests/unit/suites/profile/connection.test.ts` (238 lines).
The rerun adds `tests/unit/suites/json-rpc-type-guards.test.ts` at 285 lines, violating the mission and skill 200-line file limit.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit final diff review step that rejects package/manifest version bumps outside release work, enforces the 200-line limit for every touched file (including tests), and forbids `followedProcedure: true` unless the full documented validation sequence is actually recorded in the handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Rerun reviews for `fix-runtime-messages-test-coverage`, `fix-profile-test-coverage`, and `fix-json-rpc-test-coverage` all found forbidden version bumps; the latter two also shipped over-limit test files, and all three handoffs still implied procedure compliance despite missing parts of the documented validation sequence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how no-op fixes should record provenance: when the defect was already resolved earlier in branch history, workers should cite the actual resolving commit (or explicitly mark the fix as no-op) rather than attaching the current HEAD commit to the handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-orchestrator-planstatus-alignment` review passed on current code state, but the worker session made no code changes and still reported unrelated HEAD commit `75fbc10` as the fix commit, obscuring where the alignment actually landed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten the version-cleanup guardrail to say that when a fix must preserve an exact historical version, workers should bypass auto-bumping hooks (`--no-verify` or equivalent) instead of relying on staging only the version files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-version-bump-issues` worker staged only `package.json` and the two manifests, but the pre-commit hook rebumped them from `0.4.60` to `0.4.61`, leaving the original blocking issue unresolved.

rejectedObservations.observation: Runtime-message shared-state docs still disagree about whether the entrypoint is `runtime-message-definitions.ts` or `runtime-messages.ts` and whether there are 21 or 22 variants.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented and partly mission-scoped; `.factory/library/shared-foundation-notes.md` already records the current runtime-message structure, and updating mission contract/docs is outside this validator run.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/library/architecture.md` still points to removed shared provider/layout files such as `providers.ts` and older orchestrator structure.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate with existing library coverage in `.factory/library/shared-foundation-notes.md`; no additional low-risk service/library update was required during this rerun.
